title: Trump celebrates the 250th anniversary of the USA amid controversies and the largest fireworks show
summary: The president delivers a speech about the 'golden age' while white supremacist marches and extreme weather mark the holiday.

title: Maximum alert for torrential rains and linear precipitation bands in northern Kyushu
summary: The active seasonal front causes risk of landslides and floods, while nine years pass since the Asakura tragedy.

title: France, Morocco and Argentina advance to quarter-finals on a vibrant World Cup day
summary: Mbappé ties Messi in the scoring table, Morocco dominates Canada, and Argentina struggles to overcome surprise team Cape Verde.

title: Japanese dancer Ryoma Gzherelev wins gold medal at the Moscow International Ballet Competition
summary: He is the first Japanese to achieve this prestigious award in 17 years, also excelling in the youth category.

title: The rejuvenation of the taxi sector in Japan, driven by mobility apps
summary: The popularization of transportation apps facilitates the entry of young drivers and improves profitability in large cities.

title: Yoshinobu Yamamoto earns his ninth win and is selected to the MLB All-Star Game
summary: The Dodgers pitcher dominates the Padres with 10 strikeouts and will be the Japanese representative alongside Shohei Ohtani at the All-Star Game.

title: The Hayabusa2 probe successfully performs a high-speed flyby of asteroid Torifune
summary: The Japanese spacecraft passes within a few kilometers of the celestial body at 5 km per second to continue its exploration mission.

title: Nagoya's Kinko Gakuin Women's University announces its conversion to coeducation by 2029
summary: The institution seeks to contribute to closing the gender gap by training talents without distinction of sex in a historic change.

title: Senior official of international criminal organization 'Prince G' arrested again in Japan
summary: He is accused of illegally lending his residence card, in an operation against one of the largest mafias in Asia.

title: Four candidates compete for the governorship of Shiga in elections with low turnout
summary: The current governor and three aspirants vie for the position in an election where abstention could determine the outcome.

title: Steller sea lion pup born at the Marinepia Nihonkai aquarium in Niigata
summary: This is the fifth successful birth of the species at the park and has caused a sensation among visitors due to its cuteness.

title: Trump contacts leaders of Russia, Ukraine and Israel by phone on the eve of the NATO summit
summary: The former president seeks to mediate in international conflicts and prepare his agenda ahead of the Atlantic Alliance summit.

title: Criticism of the Takaiichi government's management increases over its legislative agenda and leadership style
summary: The opposition questions the reduction of parliamentary seats and the 'vice capital' plan while analysts point to a decline in popularity.

title: Shinzo Abe's widow, Akie, presides over an event in memory of the former prime minister four years after his assassination
summary: At the ceremony, she urged to 'make the seed flourish' left by the Japanese leader assassinated during a political rally.

title: Miki Sudo wins New York hot dog eating contest for the twelfth time, but without world record
summary: The American champion could not surpass her own record of 48 sausages, but dominated the women's competition.

title: China announces joint military exercises with Russia and it is feared that some units could surround Japan
summary: The Chinese Defense Minister confirmed the maneuvers, which will end with the deployment of ships near Japanese waters.

title: France exceeds 40 degrees and housing under historic roofs aggravates the situation of the most vulnerable
summary: The extreme heat wave turns attics into uninhabitable spaces, increasing the risk for the population without resources.

title: Micron begins construction of a new semiconductor plant in Hiroshima with an investment of 1.5 trillion yen
summary: The factory will produce next-generation memory chips, strengthening the semiconductor supply chain in Japan.

title: Japanese government will ask local governments for a total review of vulnerabilities against cyberattacks with AI
summary: The measure seeks to strengthen national security against the growing threat of automated attacks driven by artificial intelligence.

title: Japan's currency intervention loses effectiveness against the huge speculative bets of hedge funds
summary: Massive 'yen selling' moves by currency traders counteract the measures of the Bank of Japan and the Ministry of Finance.
